title: A novel approach to forecast global CO2 emission using Bat and Cuckoo optimization algorithms
author: Bahmani, GhasemiNejad, et al.

steps.title: Optimization and method validation
steps.instruction: Application of Bat algorithm (BA) together with Cuckoo Optimization Algorithm (COA) techniques to estimate the Global CO2 emission is the main objective of this paper.

steps.title: Optimization and method validation
steps.instruction: The fitness function for selecting candidates for optimal coefficients is defined as:(11)MinF(Z)=‚àëj=1k(Yobserved‚àíYpredicted)2Where Yobserved and Ypredicted are the observed and predicted Global CO2 emission respectively, and k is the number of observations. [11]

steps.title: Optimization and method validation
steps.instruction: In addition, error metrics MSE,1 MAE,2 RMSE3 were used to compare the performance of linear and exponential models to provide the best data analysis. These errors are specified in the forms of Eqs.¬Ý(12)-(14)(12)MSE=1n‚àëi=1n(yobservedi‚àíypredictedi)2(13)MAE=1n‚àëi=1n|yobservedi‚àíypredictedi|(14)RMSE=1n‚àëi=1n(yobservedi‚àíypredictedi)2

steps.title: Optimization and method validation
steps.instruction: Global CO2 emission from 1980 to 2018 is considered as a case in point in this paper. The available data is partly used for finding the optimal, or near optimal, values of the weighting parameters (1980‚Äì2013) and partly for validating the methods (2014‚Äì2018). [12]

steps.title: Optimization and method validation
steps.instruction: Following steps are conducted for estimating Global CO2 emission between 1980 and 2018:

steps.title: Optimization and method validation
steps.instruction: Step 1: natural gas, global oil, primary energy, coal consumption and Global CO2 emission need normalizing according to Eq.¬Ý(15):(15)ZN=(ZR‚àíZmin)/(Zmax‚àíZmin)

steps.title: Optimization and method validation
steps.instruction: ZN: Normalized value, ZR: The value to be normalized, Zmin: The minimum value in all the values for related variable, Zmax: The maximum value in all the values for related variable. The Zmin and Zmax values for each variable are selected between 1980 and 2013 and are shown in Table¬Ý1.Table 1Values for normalization.Table 1ZminZmaxglobal oil consumption (Million tonnes)2841.44350.3natural gas (Billion cubic meters)1224.22897.5coal (Million tonnes oil equivalent)1793.33867primary energy consumption (Million tonnes oil equivalent)6507.412,819.4Global CO2 emission (Million tonnes of carbon dioxide)17,976.732,799.9

steps.title: Optimization and method validation
steps.instruction: Step 2: The proposed methods are used in order to determine corresponding weighting factors (Ci) for each model according to the lowest objective functions. [11]

steps.title: Optimization and method validation
steps.instruction: Step 3: The best results of step 2 for each model and less average relative errors in the testing period are chosen (i.e., the related data from 2014 to 2018). The best-obtained weighting factors by COA and BA for linear and exponential models are shown in Table¬Ý2.Table 2The best-obtained weighting factors by COA and BA optimization algorithms.Table 2Algorithm ModelC1C2C3C4C5C6C7C8C9YBA‚àíGCO2linear0.27600.17970.29210.01290.1217‚Äì‚Äì‚Äì‚ÄìYBA‚àíGCO2exponential0.09040.01440.10530.01360.44370.12460.26890.00340.4270YCOA‚àíGCO2linear0.03870.28480.4330.00020.1228‚Äì‚Äì‚Äì‚ÄìYCOA‚àíGCO2exponential0.19970.02130.19480.00740.42310.08030.72340.30100.6827

steps.title: Optimization and method validation
steps.instruction: The obtained values of MSE, RMSE and MAE for both methods are presented in Table¬Ý3.Table 3Comparison of the Performance evaluation of linear, exponential COA and BA optimization algorithms performance criteria.Table 3Algorithm ModelMSERMSEMAEYBA‚àíGCO2linear0.09950.31550.2595YBA‚àíGCO2exponential0.08980.29960.2322YCOA‚àíGCO2linear0.09370.30610.2469YCOA‚àíGCO2exponential0.04040.20110.1865

steps.title: Optimization and method validation
steps.instruction: It can be seen that there is a good agreement between the results obtained from BA Global CO2 emission models (BA‚àíGCO2) and COA Global CO2 emission models (COA‚àíGCO2) but COA- exponential model outperformed the other models.

steps.title: Optimization and method validation
steps.instruction: Tables¬Ý4 and 5 for the modeling and the testing data show the performance of the Bat and cuckoo optimization algorithm for all models.
